mission.title: Install and Configure RabbitMQ Cluster Operator
mission.description: The RabbitMQ Cluster Operator is a Kubernetes operator designed to manage RabbitMQ clusters, providing lifecycle management including creation, upgrade, and graceful shutdown. It simplifies the deployment of RabbitMQ in Kubernetes environments, but requires familiarity with Kubernetes custom resources and operators.

steps.title: Add the RabbitMQ Cluster Operator Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the RabbitMQ Cluster Operator Helm repository to your local Helm configuration.

```bash

helm repo add rabbitmq https://rabbitmq.github.io/cluster-operator

```

> This repository contains the necessary charts for installation.

steps.title: Install the RabbitMQ Cluster Operator
steps.description:
This command installs the RabbitMQ Cluster Operator in the 'rabbitmq-system' namespace.

```bash

kubectl create namespace rabbitmq-system

helm install rabbitmq-operator rabbitmq/cluster-operator --namespace rabbitmq-system --version 2.20.0

```

> Create a dedicated namespace for the operator.
> Install the operator using Helm.

steps.title: Deploy a RabbitMQ cluster
steps.description:
This step deploys a sample RabbitMQ cluster using a predefined YAML configuration.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/rabbitmq/cluster-operator/main/docs/examples/hello-world/rabbitmq.yaml

```

> Deploy the example RabbitMQ cluster.

steps.title: Verify the RabbitMQ cluster is running
steps.description:
Check the status of the RabbitMQ pods to ensure they are running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n rabbitmq-system

kubectl get rabbitmqclusters -n rabbitmq-system

```

> List all pods in the rabbitmq-system namespace.
> Verify the status of the RabbitMQ cluster custom resource.

uninstall.title: Uninstall the RabbitMQ Cluster Operator
uninstall.description:
This step removes the RabbitMQ Cluster Operator and all associated resources. Be aware that this will delete the RabbitMQ cluster and any data stored within it.

```bash

helm uninstall rabbitmq-operator --namespace rabbitmq-system

kubectl delete namespace rabbitmq-system

```

> Uninstall the RabbitMQ Cluster Operator.
> Delete the namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade the RabbitMQ Cluster Operator
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you backup your RabbitMQ data. This step will upgrade the operator to the latest version.

```bash

kubectl get rabbitmqclusters -n rabbitmq-system -o yaml > backup-rabbitmqclusters.yaml

helm upgrade rabbitmq-operator rabbitmq/cluster-operator --namespace rabbitmq-system --version 2.20.0

kubectl get pods -n rabbitmq-system

```

> Backup the current RabbitMQ cluster configuration.
> Upgrade the RabbitMQ Cluster Operator.
> Verify that the pods are running after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl get rabbitmqclusters -n rabbitmq-system -o yaml > backup-rabbitmqclusters.yaml

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to create RabbitMQ cluster'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur due to insufficient resources or misconfiguration.

**Fix:**
Check the resource limits and ensure your cluster has enough CPU and memory allocated.

troubleshooting.title: Pods are stuck in 'Pending' state
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This typically indicates that there are not enough resources available in the cluster.

**Fix:**
Verify the node resource availability and consider scaling your nodes.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'RabbitMQ cluster not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This error indicates that the specified RabbitMQ cluster resource does not exist.

**Fix:**
Ensure that the RabbitMQ cluster resource is correctly defined and applied in the correct namespace.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Connection refused' when accessing RabbitMQ
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may happen if the RabbitMQ service is not properly exposed or the pods are not running.

**Fix:**
Check the service configuration and ensure that the RabbitMQ pods are in a running state.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for RabbitMQ 4.1.3, improved CRD validation, and enhanced logging.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see the RabbitMQ pods running with a status of 'Running' when executing 'kubectl get pods -n rabbitmq-system'.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a running Kubernetes cluster and the necessary tools installed.
